No Poverty.reason: Course discusses financial services and innovation, indirectly linking to economic resources for the poor (Target 1.4).

Quality Education.reason: Course aims to enhance professional competence and understanding of social responsibility (Target 4.7).

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: Focus on innovation and entrepreneurship in financial services aligns with economic growth (Target 8.3).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: Course emphasizes technological innovation and its impact on industries (Target 9.2).

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: Course examines laws and regulations, linking to justice and legal frameworks (Target 16.3).

No Poverty.reason: Inferred link to Target 1.4 (access to financial services) through FinTech topics like "P2P Lending," which can serve as a form of microfinance for the poor.

Quality Education.reason: The course is a form of tertiary education providing "advanced professional knowledge" and skills for "entrepreneurship," aligning with Targets 4.3 and 4.4.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: Core focus on "innovation and entrepreneurship" and "financial services" through FinTech, directly supporting economic growth and job creation (Targets 8.2, 8.3, 8.10).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course is fundamentally about "technological innovation" and "FinTech," directly addressing industry innovation and technology development (Targets 9.3, 9.5, 9.b).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: FinTech can promote financial inclusion (Target 10.2). The course examines how technology can "responsibly address market shortcomings" and improve financial regulation (Target 10.5).

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: A tangential link through "Sharing economy," which can relate to sustainable transport systems (Target 11.2), though the course focus is financial and legal, not urban planning.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The "Sharing economy" promotes efficient resource use (Target 12.2). The course explicitly includes "social responsibility and sustainability issues" as a core competency.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: Strong focus on "The role of law," "regulation and compliance," and "PII laws," which supports the rule of law and transparent institutions (Targets 16.3, 16.6, 16.10).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: Discusses the role of "Venture Capital (VC) firms" in funding innovation, a form of multi-stakeholder partnership mobilizing financial resources for technology (Targets 17.3, 17.17).
